methodology: GW38 final gameweek — all 10 fixtures play simultaneously. features.json has fdr=3 uniformly (ETL end-of-season stub). Per-player fixture context applied from GW38 2025/26 schedule. Away fixtures penalised +0.3 to weighted FDR. No DGW or BGW is structurally possible in GW38.

squad_fixture_ranking.name: Aina
squad_fixture_ranking.note: NFO home vs Chelsea. Forest solid at home. Best DEF clean sheet candidate in squad — and only team with non-zero xpts signal (via Sels).

squad_fixture_ranking.name: Wood
squad_fixture_ranking.note: Same NFO home fixture. Wood leads the line; goal threat in a winnable home game.

squad_fixture_ranking.name: Anderson
squad_fixture_ranking.note: NFO MID, same home game. NFO triple-up is the best fixture cluster in the squad.

squad_fixture_ranking.name: Gabriel
squad_fixture_ranking.note: ARS home vs relegated Everton. Excellent fixture but Gabriel is DOUBTFUL — fitness risk destroys the upside. If he plays, strong CS + set-piece goal potential.

squad_fixture_ranking.name: Raya
squad_fixture_ranking.note: ARS GK home vs relegated Everton. Strong CS candidate.

squad_fixture_ranking.name: Rogers
squad_fixture_ranking.note: AVL home vs Newcastle. Standard fixture, moderate CS/return ceiling.

squad_fixture_ranking.name: M.Salah
squad_fixture_ranking.note: LIV home vs Crystal Palace. Excellent fixture — Salah's individual ceiling makes this a strong captaincy contender despite current vice captain role.

squad_fixture_ranking.name: Mbeumo
squad_fixture_ranking.note: BRE home vs Man City. Tough fixture for Mbeumo — though if City rotate heavily (likely in GW38), this gets softer. Monitor City team news.

squad_fixture_ranking.name: Bowen
squad_fixture_ranking.note: WHU AWAY at Tottenham. Current captain. Away trip with FDR 3.3 is a fixture downgrade — weaker than NFO home or LIV home. Captain choice is questionable on fixture grounds alone.

squad_fixture_ranking.name: Mateta
squad_fixture_ranking.note: CRY AWAY at Liverpool. Worst attacking fixture in the starting XI. Low return ceiling.

squad_fixture_ranking.name: Gvardiol
squad_fixture_ranking.note: MCI AWAY at Brentford. Fixture is manageable but Man City rotation risk in GW38 dead rubber is HIGH. If league position is settled, Guardiola will rest players. Confirm team news Saturday morning.

squad_fixture_ranking.name: Sels
squad_fixture_ranking.note: Bench GK. Best fixture of any GK in the pool (NFO home). Only player in xpts.json with xpts > 0 (xpts=1). Emergency cover if Raya is injured.

squad_fixture_ranking.name: Haaland
squad_fixture_ranking.note: UNAVAILABLE — confirmed out. Zero points contribution. Dead bench asset.

squad_fixture_ranking.name: Munoz
squad_fixture_ranking.note: Bench DEF. CRY away at Liverpool — worst defensive fixture. Low CS probability.

squad_fixture_ranking.name: Kluivert
squad_fixture_ranking.note: Bench MID. BOU home vs Wolves — good fixture on paper. Rotation risk for Kluivert himself.

best_runs.reason: Home fixture with only team showing positive xpts in the dataset (Sels xpts=1). Best defensive clean sheet cluster and consistent attacking threat. NFO triple-up is the strongest fixture group.
Liverpool home vs Crystal Palace. Salah's individual ceiling in a soft home fixture makes this the best single-player attacking opportunity in the squad. Strong captaincy argument.
Arsenal home vs relegated Everton. Excellent fixture but Gabriel's doubtful status creates starting XI uncertainty. Raya CS upside is real if Arsenal keep it tight.

worst_runs.reason: Away at Anfield — worst attacking fixture in the squad. Low return probability for Mateta. Munoz on bench faces same CS problem.
Brentford host Man City. Hard fixture for Mbeumo's returns unless City rotate significantly.
Away at Tottenham with the armband. WHU have limited motivation in GW38 and face a harder fixture than several alternatives in the squad.

dgw_alerts.detail: GW38 is the simultaneous season finale. All 20 clubs play exactly once. Double gameweeks are structurally impossible. No squad player benefits from a double.

bgw_alerts.detail: Every team plays GW38. All 15 squad players have one fixture. Zero blanks.

rotation_risks.reason: Man City's final day — if their season objective (title/CL place) is secured, Guardiola rotates heavily. Gvardiol is a rotation target. Confirm team news before deadline.
Mbeumo is Brentford's key asset, unlikely to be rested, but fixture vs City is tough regardless.

injury_flags.impact: Starting XI risk. If Gabriel misses out, ARS DEF cover is lost entirely. Raya still plays but no Gabriel set-piece goal upside.
Bench only, confirmed out. Zero contribution. Dead bench slot.

captaincy_alert: Current captain Bowen faces WHU AWAY at Tottenham (weighted FDR 3.3). M.Salah (VC) has LIV HOME vs Crystal Palace (weighted FDR 2.0) — significantly better fixture and comparable individual ceiling. On fixture grounds, Salah is the recommended captain switch.
best_def_run: NFO (Aina) HOME vs Chelsea — best defensive fixture. ARS (Gabriel/Raya) HOME vs Everton is close but Gabriel doubtful status reduces confidence.
best_attack_run: LIV (M.Salah) HOME vs Crystal Palace — best single attacking asset with softest relevant fixture.
summary: GW38. No DGW or BGW — every squad player has exactly one fixture. Best runs: NFO triple (Aina/Wood/Anderson HOME vs CHE, weighted FDR 2.0) and LIV (Salah HOME vs CRY, FDR 2.0). Worst: CRY away at Anfield (Mateta, FDR 4.3), BRE home vs City (Mbeumo, FDR 4.0). Captain Bowen (WHU AWAY Spurs, FDR 3.3) is a fixture downgrade vs vice-captain Salah (LIV HOME vs CRY, FDR 2.0). Gabriel DOUBTFUL risk flagged. Haaland confirmed UNAVAILABLE (bench dead). Gvardiol faces HIGH rotation risk with Man City in a potential GW38 dead rubber.
